description_long: This object consists of three large, weathered wooden crates stacked in a staggered arrangement. Each crate is constructed from horizontal wooden planks with reinforced, darker wooden edges and visible metal corner brackets, giving them a rugged, industrial look. The tops of the crates display a circular stamp or logo, suggesting they are used for shipping or storage, and some sides feature adhesive shipping labels. The wood appears aged, with some greenish discoloration at the corners, possibly from exposure to moisture or outdoor conditions. The crates have open slats near the top edges, likely for ventilation or ease of handling. Their robust build and utilitarian design make them suitable for use in urban environments such as warehouses, garages, or loading docks.
description: A group of three large, weathered wooden crates with reinforced corners, shipping labels, and stamped tops, stacked in a rugged, industrial style.
description_view_0: This view shows the front and top faces of two crates, highlighting the wood grain, reinforced corners, and the circular stamp on the top surfaces.
description_view_1: This angle reveals the staggered stacking of the crates, with the top crate's front and side visible, and the open slats near the top edges more apparent.
description_view_2: This perspective emphasizes the side of the top crate and the front faces of the lower crates, with the greenish weathering on the corners and the shipping label on the side.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the shipping labels on the side of the top crate and the front faces of the lower crates, with the stacked arrangement clearly visible.
